meta.note: All decisions in this file are closed. Do not re-litigate any decision without the founder explicitly stating "reopen [decision ID]". If a request would implicitly reopen a closed decision, surface that before proceeding.

product.name: Meridian
product.tagline: Privacy-first, local-first calendar for Apple platforms
product.sync: Tailscale only — peer mesh, 1–3 personal devices. No cloud. No server. No iCloud sync.
product.stack: SwiftUI throughout. No Electron. No React Native. No cross-platform bridges.
product.future: Rust translation layer — cross-platform, fully open source. Built after Meridian ships and generates revenue. Not in v1 scope.

build_sequence.note: Layer 1 is a hard blocker. No Layer 2 or Layer 3 code until Layer 1 passes its checklist.

layer.name: Tailscale + CRDT Substrate
layer.step: Document initialization, serialization, encrypted local persistence
Local HTTP server on port 47301 bound to Tailscale interface
Peer discovery and binary delta sync protocol

layer.name: Calendar Core UI
layer.step: iPad layout and Stage Manager contract

theory.source: Lisa Feldman Barrett — How Emotions Are Made (2017)

constructs.construct: Brain generates top-down predictions about internal body state. Conscious affect is the brain's best guess, updated by incoming signals. (PMC5390700 §2)
When afferent signals deviate from predictions, a prediction error is generated. Large or repeated errors trigger allostatic recalibration. (PMC5390700 §3.1)
Failure to successfully predict and prepare is experienced as effortfulness or depletion — not as a named emotion. (PMC5390700 §3.3)
Brain weights signals by estimated reliability. Maps to the clarity dimension. Low clarity = low precision on current interoceptive signals. (PMC5390700 §2.3)

Emotion words are constructions the brain uses to categorize continuous affect. Meridian has no access to the user's emotion categories. It has access to three continuous dimensions only. It must never name an emotion. (Barrett, Ch. 1–2; PMC5390700 §1)

time_window_buckets.note: Midnight–05:00 assigned to previous day's wXXX-evening bucket.

decision.title: Minimum check-ins before baseline computed
decision.value: 7 check-ins per bucket before any baseline is computed. Below this: FORMING state. No prediction error computed.
decision.rationale: Insufficient prior experience cannot form a reliable predictive model (Barrett §2).

decision.title: Rolling baseline window
decision.value: Most recent 28 check-ins in the bucket. Older check-ins retained in AffectDoc for export only.
decision.rationale: Balances responsiveness to life change vs. noise; approx 4–6 week update cycle per bucket.

decision.title: Stability threshold
decision.value: Bucket marked STABLE when: σ_V < 0.30 AND σ_A < 0.30 AND σ_C < 0.25 If any dimension exceeds threshold: FORMING_EXTENDED — no prediction error computed. FORMING, FORMING_EXTENDED, STABLE are internal only. No UI indication.
decision.rationale: ~2–3 SD spread per dimension; proportional to each dimension's range.

decision.title: Prediction error formula
decision.value: ε = √( (V_new − μ_V)² + (A_new − μ_A)² + (C_new − μ_C)² ) Maximum possible ε = √(4 + 4 + 1) = 3.0 Euclidean distance in 3D affect space between new check-in and bucket rolling mean.
decision.rationale: Standard distance metric; computationally simple; interpretable.

decision.title: Significance threshold
decision.value: ε_threshold = 0.85 (~28% of maximum distance of 3.0). ε ≥ 0.85 = high-error check-in for that bucket.
decision.rationale: ~2–3 SD combined deviation. Consistent with Barrett's allostatic recalibration criterion.

decision.title: Prompt trigger
decision.value: count(high-error check-ins in same bucket, within rolling 7-day window) ≥ 3 Rolling window: from timestamp of most recent check-in in bucket, looking backward. Not calendar week.
decision.rationale: Repetition criterion from Barrett; single outliers are noise, not pattern.

decision.title: Cooldown after prompt
decision.value: 14-day cooldown per bucket after prompt shown or dismissed. No further prompts for that bucket during cooldown.
decision.rationale: Prevents prompt fatigue without requiring user management.

decision.title: System response type
decision.value: Schedule review prompt is the ONLY system response. No mood intervention. No wellness suggestion. No emotion category named. Prompt placement: inline card in day view, bottom of event stack. Never modal. Never push notification. Never menu bar popover.
decision.rationale: Meridian can observe schedule load. It cannot observe emotion categories.

decision.title: Emotion word prohibition
decision.value: ZERO emotion words in all system-generated copy. No exceptions. Prohibited words (and all derivatives): stress, anxiety, burnout, overwhelm, exhaustion, tired, drained, frustrated, excited. Prohibited constructions: — Causal attribution: "because of," "due to," "from" constructions. — Obligation imperatives: "you should," "try to," "make sure." — Any sentence naming an emotion category. — Any CTA opening a mood-logging or journaling flow.
decision.rationale: Barrett: emotion categories are user-constructed, not system-detected.

decision.title: Baseline reset and decay
decision.value: Manual reset: Settings > Affect > "Reset my baseline for [bucket]" Clears high-error count and cooldown. Does not delete raw check-ins. Restores bucket to FORMING. Requires 7 new check-ins to re-stabilize. Auto-decay: no check-ins in 45 consecutive days → bucket moves to DORMANT. Re-enters FORMING on next check-in. No user action required.
decision.rationale: Supports major life transitions; auto-decay handles organic disuse.

prompt_copy.note: Exact copy. Locked. Do not paraphrase in any implementation.
prompt_copy.variant: This morning window has been running differently than your usual mornings. Your last few check-ins here landed further from your typical range. Worth a look at what's loading up before noon? [Review morning blocks →]

This afternoon pattern looks heavier than your afternoons usually do. Something in this window is landing differently than your baseline. Want to look at how much is stacking here? [Review afternoon blocks →]
This part of your weekend has been running differently lately. Your check-ins here have been outside your usual range. Worth a look at what you're holding in this time? [Review this block →]

decision.title: Conflict resolution strategy
decision.value: Automerge-Swift (JSON CRDT). Pure Swift, no FFI, ARM-native via Xcode toolchain.
decision.rationale: Only candidate satisfying: Swift-native, correct offline-first convergence, no server.

decision.title: Y.js / Yrs eliminated
decision.value: Eliminated for v1. Rust FFI bridge disproportionate for solo Apple-first build.
decision.rationale: Maintenance burden: second toolchain, breaks on Xcode updates, invisible to LLDB.
decision.revisit: If Meridian becomes multi-user and EventDoc exceeds 50MB per device — re-evaluate Yrs memory efficiency.

decision.title: SQLite + LWW eliminated
decision.value: Eliminated. Structurally broken under clock skew in peer mesh without NTP-synchronized server.
decision.rationale: Clock skew between offline devices produces silent destructive overwrites. No history. No recovery.

decision.title: Custom OT eliminated
decision.value: Eliminated. Requires a linearization point (server). Incompatible with Tailscale-only architecture.
decision.rationale: OT cannot converge without synchronous ordering. No server means no OT.

decision.title: Document structure
decision.value: Two CRDT documents per user: EventDoc and AffectDoc. Separate concerns, separate compaction.

field.name: modifiedAt
field.note: informational only — NOT used for conflict resolution

field.name: title, location, notes
field.behavior: Register: deterministic winner; loser retained in history

field.name: startDate, endDate
field.behavior: Register: deterministic winner; both values in history

field.name: contextProfileMappings
field.behavior: Set: additive merge — both offline additions survive

field.name: checkIns (AffectDoc)
field.behavior: Map: concurrent additions merge cleanly — unique UUIDs, no conflicts

decision.title: Sync port
decision.value: 47301 (user-configurable, exposed as "Meridian Sync Port" in Settings). Bound to Tailscale IP only.

sync_endpoints.auth: MeridianAuth header — shared secret from Keychain, exchanged at device onboarding

sync_triggers.trigger: App foreground — if last sync > 5 minutes ago

decision.title: Compaction
decision.value: Triggers: — App background, if last compaction > 7 days ago. — After successful Tailscale sync, if document grew > 20% since last compaction. — Explicit: Settings > Storage > "Compact Meridian data". What it does: calls compact() on EventDoc and AffectDoc. Prunes unreachable history. Retains last 30 days of operation history. Does not delete user-visible data. Post-compaction targets: Year 1 ~1.5–2.5 MB; Year 3 ~4.5–7.5 MB.

decision.title: Schema evolution
decision.value: Additive changes (new optional field): increment schemaVersion; nil default; no migration required. v1.0 devices preserve unknown fields as opaque CRDT state. Non-additive changes (rename, type change, removal): increment schemaVersion to N; run migrate(doc:from:to:) on app launch if local schemaVersion < N. Cross-version sync warning copy (exact): "This device is running an older version of Meridian. Some events may not display all fields until you update."

decision.title: Revisit trigger
decision.value: If compacted EventDoc exceeds 25 MB on any device: evaluate annual segmentation first, then Yrs migration.

decision.title: Cross-version sync
decision.value: v1.0 device receiving v1.1 document: preserve unknown fields, show migration warning. No data corruption.

decision.title: Local at-rest crypto primitive
decision.value: CryptoKit ChaChaPoly (AEAD) — same posture rationale as D2-01/D2-02. Pure Swift surface, no FFI, no second toolchain, Apple-maintained, hardware-accelerated on Apple Silicon. The global libsodium default is overridden here for the same reason Yrs was eliminated in D2-02: a C library behind a Swift FFI bridge contradicts Meridian's Apple-only, pure-Swift posture. libsodium remains the correct choice for cross-platform / server-adjacent contexts (Secure Pride, Praxis-Aegis); not this one. Key material: 256-bit symmetric key generated via SecRandomCopyBytes on first launch and stored in Keychain (kSecAttrAccessibleAfterFirstUnlockThisDeviceOnly — usable for background sync after first unlock, never escrowed to iCloud Keychain).
decision.rationale: Apple-platforms-only Swift package; D2-01/D2-02 posture extension.

decision.title: Encryption granularity
decision.value: Encrypt the full Automerge save() output as opaque bytes. Never field-encrypt the CRDT payload. File format on disk: magic(4) "MRDN" ‖ version(1) ‖ nonce(12) ‖ ciphertext+tag. Rationale for opaque-bytes wrapping: — Preserves D2-08 / D2-10 forward-compatibility: a v1.0 reader decrypts a v1.1 blob, hands the plaintext to Automerge, and Automerge transparently preserves unknown fields as opaque CRDT state. Field-level encryption would force the storage layer to understand the schema, breaking forward-compat. — Preserves Automerge's compaction guarantees: save() output is already deduplicated and compacted; AEAD wrapping doesn't perturb the binary. — Single AEAD authentication tag covers the entire document; tampering with any byte fails decryption deterministically.
decision.rationale: Forward-compatibility (D2-08, D2-10) requires the storage layer to be schema-blind.

decision.title: Definition
decision.value: Cultural competence = the system does not require users to distort, hide, simplify, or perform their lives differently in order to use the calendar effectively. Not a values statement. A testable architectural property.

decision.title: Context profile labels
decision.value: User-editable strings. Five named defaults. Internal identifier is a stable UUID. The label is a separate editable string — not an enum. Defaults: Developer, Ops, Student, Learner, Minimal. Users can rename any label. Profile behavior travels with the UUID, not the label. A rename does not produce a merge conflict — Automerge resolves the label string; profile identity (UUID) stays intact.
decision.closes: v1 spec Open Question 2; Document 3 Code Audit Item 3

decision.title: Five testable UX properties
decision.value: 1. Role non-exclusivity: single event assignable to multiple profiles; no hierarchical copy. 2. Profile label non-othering: no label implies "normal" vs. specialty accommodation. 3. Enrichment non-normalization: no content implicitly positions one cultural paradigm as correct. 4. Temporal home screen universal readability: three time states work for non-conventional schedules. 5. Affect check-in non-prescriptiveness: no visual or copy treatment implying a "correct" affect position. Each property has explicit pass/fail criteria in Document 3.

decision.title: Enrichment unlocks
decision.value: Through use and affect data thresholds only. Never payment. Level 0 (0–13 points): affect check-in only. No enrichment content. Level 1 (≥14 points across ≥3 time windows): reflection strip + affect-matched quotes. Level 2 (≥30 points across ≥5 buckets over ≥7 days): inline capacity meter + context hints. Level 3 (≥60 points over ≥21 days, stable baseline): weekly pattern card + load review flow. UI signal for each unlock: feature simply appears. No badges. No popups.

prohibited_copy.note: Apply to all system-generated strings. Zero exceptions.

code_rules.rule: Every function fully implemented. No TODOs, stubs, or placeholder comments.
Separate concerns strictly: UI / logic / data / sync live in separate files.
No hardcoded secrets. Keychain for shared secrets and sensitive tokens.
No SQL string concatenation. Parameterized queries only.
No eval(), no innerHTML with untrusted content.
No UserDefaults for sensitive data. Keychain for secrets; encrypted local store for affect data.
HTTPS only for any external calls (rare; enrichment content is bundled).
code_rules.response_format: Every code response must include in this order: 1. Assumptions (bullet list of key inferences) 2. What's being built (1–2 sentences) 3. Code block(s) 4. Explanation (structure rationale, watch-outs) 5. Usage example (copy-paste ready) 6. Testing checklist (3–5 concrete verification steps)
code_rules.coda: End every response with: ⬡ Meridian · Layer [N] — [Layer name] · [Current surface or decision in focus] Append [CLOSED] if response closes a decision. Append [NEEDS DECISION] if response surfaces an open question requiring founder input.

accessibility.standard: WCAG 2.1 AA minimum. Non-negotiable. Not a post-MVP item.

per_component_requirements.requirement: Dynamic Type: behavior at XXXL size — no truncation, no overlap
Reduce Motion: alternative animation or none
Cognitive load: max simultaneous decisions on screen = 3

rules.rule: Color is NEVER the sole indicator of state, status, or context profile
No VoiceOver label that uses an emotion word or implies affect evaluation

privacy.rule: Affect data: local only. Never transmitted. Never displayed as a score or dimensional readout.
No engagement metrics: no streaks, no completion percentages, no "you were productive" copy.
No enrichment content requiring a network call at display time.
Enrichment content: bundled or permanently cached after one user-initiated download.

prohibitions.prohibition: Do not suggest iCloud sync, cloud sync, or any server-side component.
Do not use emotion category words in any system-facing copy.
Do not re-open closed decisions without being explicitly asked.
Do not generate UI where color alone indicates state, status, or profile.
Do not add enrichment features requiring a network call at display time.
Do not position accessibility as a post-MVP consideration.
Do not generate Layer 2 or Layer 3 code while Layer 1 tests are failing.
